content: In Proverbs 3:5-6, 'Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.' What does the Hebrew word for 'trust' mean?
explanation: Batah (בטח) means 'to trust, to lean upon' — not just to believe but to rely fully.
options: Batah — to trust, to lean upon
Aman — to believe
Yachal — to hope
Chasah — to take refuge

content: In Proverbs 31:10, 'An excellent wife who can find?' What does the Hebrew word for 'excellent' mean?
explanation: Chayil (חיל) means 'strength, might' — not merely talent but inner strength.
options: Chakam — wise
Tov — good
Chayil — strength, might
Yashar — upright

content: According to Proverbs 1:7, where does wisdom begin?
explanation: Proverbs 1:7 — The fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge. All true wisdom flows from God.
options: Experience
Education
The fear of the LORD
Following the law

content: Proverbs 3:5 teaches: 'Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on ___'
explanation: Proverbs 3:5-6 — Trust in the LORD wholly; do not lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge Him.
options: Other people
Your own strength
Wealth
Your own understanding

content: What does Proverbs 22:6 teach about raising children?
explanation: Proverbs 22:6 — The principle of training: instruct a child from youth in the way of the LORD.
options: Let children develop on their own
Be strict
Train up a child in the way he should go; even when he is old he will not depart from it
Give children full freedom

content: Proverbs 27:17 says: 'Iron sharpens iron, and ___ sharpens his friend'
explanation: Proverbs 27:17 — Iron sharpens iron; good friends help one another mature through honest relationship.
options: Heart to heart
One man sharpens another
Word against word
Face to face

content: What does Proverbs 4:23 teach we must guard above all?
explanation: Proverbs 4:23 — Keep your heart with all vigilance, for from it flow the springs of life.
options: Reputation
Health
The heart
Money

content: Proverbs urges the lazy to learn from which creature?
explanation: Proverbs 6:6 — 'Go to the ant, O sluggard.' It stores food without a commander.
options: The bird
The squirrel
The bee
The ant

content: Proverbs 11:2: 'When pride comes, then comes ___'
explanation: Proverbs 11:2 — Pride brings disgrace. Humility brings wisdom.
options: Success
Power
Disgrace
Wealth

content: Whom does Proverbs 12:1 call stupid?
explanation: Proverbs 12:1 — Whoever loves discipline loves knowledge, but whoever hates reproof is stupid.
options: Whoever hates discipline
Whoever does not study
The young
The poor

content: Proverbs 15:1 teaches: 'A soft answer ___'
explanation: Proverbs 15:1 — A soft answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
options: Wise
Turns away wrath
Calms
Brings joy

content: Proverbs 16:18: 'Pride goes before ___'
explanation: Proverbs 16:18 — Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.
options: Wisdom
Success
Destruction
Wealth

content: What does Proverbs 18:24 say about a friend?
explanation: Proverbs 18:24 — Quality over quantity. A true friend sticks closer than a brother.
options: Few friends are enough
No need for friends
Many friends are good
There is a friend who sticks closer than a brother

content: Proverbs 14:12: 'There is a way that seems right to a man, but its end is the way of ___'
explanation: Proverbs 14:12 — Not every way that looks right truly is. We need the LORD's guidance.
options: Success
Peace
Wealth
Death

content: What does Proverbs 9:10 say is the beginning of wisdom?
explanation: Proverbs 9:10 — 'The fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom, and the knowledge of the Holy One is insight.'
options: Reading many books
Accumulated experience
The fear of the LORD
Avoiding fools

content: What does Proverbs 13:24 say loving parents do for their children?
explanation: Proverbs 13:24 — 'Whoever spares the rod hates his son, but he who loves him is diligent to discipline him.'
options: Allow complete freedom
Give them everything they want
Only teach by words
Discipline them diligently (use the rod)

content: When does a friend love, according to Proverbs 17:17?
explanation: Proverbs 17:17 — 'A friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity.'
options: At all times
Only when help is needed
Only when cheerful
Only at church

content: What does Proverbs 20:1 say wine is?
explanation: Proverbs 20:1 — 'Wine is a mocker, strong drink a brawler, and whoever is led astray by it is not wise.'
options: A comfort
A mocker
A joy
A blessing

content: What does Proverbs 25:21-22 teach about an enemy when hungry?
explanation: Proverbs 25:21-22 — 'If your enemy is hungry, give him bread to eat, and if he is thirsty, give him water to drink, for you will heap burning coals on his head.'
options: Drive him away
Ignore him
Give him food and drink
Punish him

content: What does Proverbs 30:5 say about every word of God?
explanation: Proverbs 30:5 — 'Every word of God proves true; he is a shield to those who take refuge in him.'
options: Sometimes wrong
Depends on circumstance
Only for the Jews
Proves true; He is a shield to refuge-seekers

content: According to Proverbs 1:8-9, whom must a son hear? What are father's + mother's words to him?
explanation: Proverbs 1:8-9 — 'Hear, my son, your father's instruction, and forsake not your mother's teaching, for they are a graceful garland for your head and pendants for your neck.' Father + mother as TEACHERS = honor to the son (garland + pendants). Proverbs is largely written from a FATHER teaching SON perspective.
options: Hear teacher — knowledge (as described in the passage)
Hear king — power (per the textual narrative)
Hear FATHER'S INSTRUCTION + DO NOT forsake mother's TEACHING — they are a GRACEFUL GARLAND on head + PENDANTS on neck
Hear friends — joy (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 1:10, if sinners entice you to evil, what must you do?
explanation: Proverbs 1:10 — 'My son, if sinners entice you, do not consent.' Very short but core: PEER PRESSURE. The enticement often sounds sweet (1:11-14) — must DETERMINEDLY REFUSE from the start. Reason: 1:18-19 — they are setting a trap for THEMSELVES.
options: Follow them
Argue with them
Silently observe
DO NOT consent

content: According to Proverbs 3:9-10, with what should you honor the LORD? Result?
explanation: Proverbs 3:9-10 — 'Honor the LORD with your wealth and with the firstfruits of all your produce; then your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will be bursting with wine.' Firstfruits (Lev 23:10) = sign of trusting God to keep providing. Principle: give FIRST — God WILL FILL. Same idea Mal 3:10.
options: Soul — God's love (as described in the passage)
WEALTH + FIRSTFRUITS of all your produce — barns will be FILLED + vats will BURST with NEW WINE
Prayer — heard (per the textual narrative)
Works — rewarded (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 6:16-19, the LORD HATES 6 (actually 7) things — abominations. What structure does the verse use?
explanation: Proverbs 6:16 — 'There are six things that the LORD hates, seven that are an abomination to him.' THE 'X / X+1' STRUCTURE = common in Proverbs (cf. 30:15, 18, 21, 29). The 7 (6:17-19): haughty EYES + lying TONGUE + hands shedding INNOCENT BLOOD + heart that DEVISES wicked plans + feet RUNNING to evil + a FALSE WITNESS who breathes lies + sower of DISCORD among brothers.
options: 'There are SIX things the LORD HATES, SEVEN that are an ABOMINATION to him'
'10 things' (per the textual narrative)
'All Lord likes' (according to the text's framing)
'Nothing Lord hates' (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 10:1, a wise son makes whom glad? A foolish son brings sorrow to whom?
explanation: Proverbs 10:1 — 'A wise son makes a glad father, but a foolish son is a sorrow to his mother.' This opens chapter 10 = 'proverbs of Solomon' (10:1-22:16). Antithesis between FATHER + MOTHER. Same idea repeated 17:21, 17:25.
options: Friend — enemy (according to the text's framing)
Father — none (per the textual narrative)
Wise son makes FATHER glad — foolish son is a SORROW to MOTHER
Community — family (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 14:34, what makes a NATION GREAT? What is REPROACH to peoples?
explanation: Proverbs 14:34 — 'Righteousness exalts a nation, but sin is a reproach to any people.' Clear principle: GREAT NATION = righteousness; SHAME = sin. NOT military strength, economy, or learning. A WARNING to all nations — not just Israel.
options: Strong army — weakness reproach
Learning — ignorance reproach
Production — poverty reproach
RIGHTEOUSNESS exalts a nation; but SIN is a REPROACH to any people

content: According to Proverbs 16:9, what does a person's heart do with WAYS? What does the LORD do with STEPS?
explanation: Proverbs 16:9 — 'The heart of man plans his way, but the LORD establishes his steps.' Paradox: MAN plans — GOD directs the steps. Does NOT say man has no will — says ultimately GOD rules. Same teaching 19:21 (many plans but Lord's purpose stands), 21:1 (king's heart in God's hand).
options: Both by man (per the textual narrative)
The HEART of man PLANS his WAY — but the LORD ESTABLISHES his STEPS
Both by God (according to the text's framing)
Both by chance (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 18:10, what is the LORD's name? What do the righteous do?
explanation: Proverbs 18:10 — 'The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous man runs into it and is safe.' NAME (shem) = God's character. TOWER (migdal) = a fortified stronghold. The righteous RUN IN — don't stand outside. Contrast next verse (18:11): the rich trust their wealth as a 'high wall' — but only in imagination, NOT secure.
options: Strength — go fight (according to the text's framing)
Glory — stand watch (as described in the passage)
The NAME of the LORD is a STRONG TOWER — the righteous RUN INTO it and are SAFE
Sound — cry out (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Proverbs 21:23, who keeps MOUTH + TONGUE keeps what?
explanation: Proverbs 21:23 — 'Whoever keeps his mouth and his tongue keeps himself out of trouble.' Principle: speak MORE = MORE risk; speak SLOWLY = LESS risk. Same idea 13:3, 17:27, James 3:5-6 (small tongue, great fire). SPIRITUAL safety is tied to TONGUE control.
options: Keeps SOUL from troubles
Keeps money
Keeps honor
Keeps health

content: According to Proverbs 22:1, what is more precious than GREAT WEALTH? Than GOLD + SILVER?
explanation: Proverbs 22:1 — 'A good name is to be chosen rather than great riches, and favor is better than silver or gold.' NAME (shem) in Hebrew = REPUTATION. Not about pride — about whether others can TRUST you. Wealth can be LOST — once reputation is lost, it CANNOT be regained. Same teaching Eccl 7:1.
options: Long life (as described in the passage)
Health (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Learning (according to the text's framing)
A GOOD NAME (reputation) is to be chosen rather than great riches — FAVOR is better than silver and gold

content: According to Proverbs 28:13, what about one who CONCEALS SIN? Who CONFESSES + FORSAKES?
explanation: Proverbs 28:13 — 'Whoever conceals his transgressions will not prosper, but he who confesses and forsakes them will obtain mercy.' Same idea as 1 John 1:9 ('if we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive'). 2 VERBS: CONFESS + FORSAKE — not just admit but abandon. Like David: hiding sin (Ps 32:3-4 'bones wasted away') vs confessing (32:5 'you forgave').
options: Conceals = blessed; confesses = punished
Both same (per the textual narrative)
Whoever CONCEALS sins will NOT PROSPER — whoever CONFESSES + FORSAKES will obtain MERCY
Conceals = wise (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 1:20-21, what does WISDOM (personified) do? Where?
explanation: Proverbs 1:20-21 — 'Wisdom cries aloud in the street, in the markets she raises her voice; at the head of the noisy streets she cries out; at the entrance of the city gates she speaks.' WISDOM personified = a woman calling (vs 'FOLLY' personified in ch 9). WISDOM IS NOT HIDDEN — God wants ALL to hear. Reason many lack wisdom: refusal to listen, not wisdom hiding.
options: Whispers in temple (per the textual narrative)
CRIES ALOUD outside + lifts voice in markets + at GATES of city — calls everyone
Quiet in garden — for king (according to the text's framing)
Silent on mountain — for prophets

content: According to Proverbs 2:6, where does WISDOM come from? Who gives KNOWLEDGE?
explanation: Proverbs 2:6 — 'For the LORD gives wisdom; from his mouth come knowledge and understanding.' WISDOM is not self-generated — IT IS FROM GOD. Source 1: 1:7 = fear of the LORD = beginning; Source 2: 2:6 = from the LORD's mouth. Also why 2:5 ('then you will understand the fear of the LORD and find the knowledge of God').
options: The LORD gives wisdom; from his MOUTH come knowledge + understanding
From human reason (according to the text's framing)
From life experience (as described in the passage)
From education (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Proverbs 3:13-15, who FINDS wisdom has what? Wisdom is more precious than?
explanation: Proverbs 3:13-15 — 'Blessed is the one who finds wisdom, and the one who gets understanding, for the gain from her is better than gain from silver and her profit better than gold. She is more precious than jewels, and nothing you desire can compare with her.' Wisdom WORTH MORE than anything — because it gives LIFE (3:18). Investment principle: forsake all to gain wisdom.
options: Has power — more than money (per the textual narrative)
Has strength — more than gold (according to the text's framing)
Has long life — more than silver (as described in the passage)
BLESSED — gain from wisdom is BETTER than silver + her revenue BETTER than GOLD; more precious than JEWELS, NOTHING you desire compares

content: According to Proverbs 3:27-28, regarding one who deserves, what NOT to do? What NOT to say to neighbor?
explanation: Proverbs 3:27-28 — 'Do not withhold good from those to whom it is due, when it is in your power to do it. Do not say to your neighbor, "Go, and come again, tomorrow I will give it" — when you have it with you.' SPECIFIC instruction on practical justice. Same teaching as Mt 5:42, James 2:15-16. Key: don't delay help.
options: Don't be rude; don't oppose (per the textual narrative)
Don't challenge; don't avenge (according to the text's framing)
DO NOT WITHHOLD GOOD from those entitled; do NOT say 'Go + come BACK tomorrow' when you have it NOW
Don't lend; don't expect return (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 4:7, what is the BEGINNING (chief) of wisdom?
explanation: Proverbs 4:7 — 'The beginning of wisdom is this: Get wisdom, and whatever you get, get insight.' '4:7 = the golden verse of Proverbs'. WISDOM = priority #1, cannot be skipped to chase other things. 'WHATEVER YOU GET' = invest all — because wisdom is WORTH IT.
options: Education — that is wisdom (as described in the passage)
WISDOM is the principal thing — therefore GET WISDOM; with all you've got GET UNDERSTANDING
Money — wisdom (per the textual narrative)
Power — wisdom (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 4:18-19, what is the PATH of the righteous LIKE? Of the wicked?
explanation: Proverbs 4:18-19 — 'But the path of the righteous is like the light of dawn, which shines brighter and brighter until full day. The way of the wicked is like deep darkness; they do not know over what they stumble.' Two opposing paths: INCREASING LIGHT (dawn → full day) vs TOTAL DARKNESS. The righteous's LIFE grows CLEARER; the wicked DON'T KNOW what's tripping them.
options: Both like dew (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Both like trees (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Both like streams (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Path of RIGHTEOUS like LIGHT of DAWN, shining brighter and brighter to FULL DAY; way of WICKED like DEEP DARKNESS, they do not know what they STUMBLE over

content: According to Proverbs 5:18-19, with whom should you REJOICE? When?
explanation: Proverbs 5:18-19 — 'Let your fountain be blessed, and rejoice in the wife of your youth, a lovely deer, a graceful doe. Let her breasts fill you at all times with delight; be intoxicated always in her love.' DELIGHT in your wife = the antidote to ADULTERY (5:1-14). Marital love is HONORED — not shameful.
options: With WIFE of your youth — be intoxicated with her LOVE
With sister — always (per the textual narrative)
With mother — when sick
With servant — when free

content: According to Proverbs 6:23, what is the commandment? Law? Reproofs of discipline?
explanation: Proverbs 6:23 — 'For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light, and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life.' 3 images: LAMP (illumination), LIGHT (brilliance), WAY (direction). Same teaching as Ps 119:105 ('Your word is a lamp to my feet'). The Law is NOT a burden — it's a LAMP showing the path.
options: Sign; book; teacher (per the textual narrative)
Rule; statute; training (as described in the passage)
Commandment is a LAMP, law is LIGHT, reproofs of DISCIPLINE are the WAY OF LIFE
Fence; camp; safety (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 6:25, what should the HEART NOT desire about the adulterous woman?
explanation: Proverbs 6:25 — 'Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes.' Lust starts in the HEART (Mt 5:28 same teaching). 'EYELASHES' = seduction by glances. NOT saying don't see — says HEART don't DESIRE. Difference between temptation + sin: sin starts when the HEART consents.
options: Don't desire her money (according to the text's framing)
Do not desire her BEAUTY in your HEART — do NOT let her CAPTURE you with her EYELASHES
Don't desire her food (per the textual narrative)
Don't desire her house (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 7:1-5, why must the SON keep the FATHER'S commands + treasure law as APPLE OF EYE?
explanation: Proverbs 7:1-5 — 'My son, keep my words and treasure up my commandments with you... that they may keep you from the forbidden woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words.' The Law = a HEDGE of protection. Story 7:6-23 — a foolish young man seduced into the adulteress's house, ends 'dead'. The warning STILL applies today.
options: For wealth (per the textual narrative)
For pretty wife (as described in the passage)
Father's joy (according to the text's framing)
To keep you from the FORBIDDEN WOMAN + ADULTERESS with smooth WORDS

content: According to Proverbs 9:1, what has WISDOM done to prepare a feast?
explanation: Proverbs 9:1-2 — 'Wisdom has built her house; she has hewn her seven pillars. She has slaughtered her beasts; she has mixed her wine; she has also set her table.' WISDOM = personified hostess. 7 PILLARS — number of PERFECTION; wisdom's house is FIRM. Contrast 9:13-18 — FOLLY also invites but to the WORLD OF DEAD.
options: BUILT a HOUSE + HEWED 7 PILLARS — slaughtered BEASTS + mixed WINE + SET her TABLE
Built school — called students (as described in the passage)
Built garden — light feast (per the textual narrative)
Built temple — called priests (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 10:12, what does HATRED stir up? What does LOVE do with sin?
explanation: Proverbs 10:12 — 'Hatred stirs up strife, but love covers all offenses.' NOT saying love is foolishly blind to sin — saying it doesn't get IRRITATED by small offenses day after day. 1 Pet 4:8 quotes this. 'COVERING SINS' does NOT mean condoning evil — means OVERLOOKING small relational failures.
options: Both expose sin (according to the text's framing)
Both hide sin (per the textual narrative)
HATRED stirs up STRIFE — LOVE COVERS all OFFENSES
Both praise sin (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 10:19, who SPEAKS MUCH? Who RESTRAINS LIPS?
explanation: Proverbs 10:19 — 'When words are many, transgression is not lacking, but whoever restrains his lips is prudent.' MORE WORDS = MORE chances of SAYING wrong. Same idea Eccl 5:2-3 ('not rash with mouth + not many words before God'), James 1:19 (quick to hear, slow to speak).
options: Talkative wise — silent foolish (as described in the passage)
Both same (per the textual narrative)
Talkative rich — silent poor (according to the text's framing)
When WORDS are MANY, transgression is not lacking; whoever RESTRAINS his lips is PRUDENT

content: According to Proverbs 11:14, without GUIDANCE the people are what? Many COUNSELORS?
explanation: Proverbs 11:14 — 'Where there is no guidance, a people falls, but in an abundance of counselors there is safety.' Same idea 15:22 ('without counsel, plans fail'), 24:6 ('in much counsel there is victory'). Sole leadership = risk; many VOICES = safety.
options: Still prosper — counselors confuse
PEOPLE FALL — VICTORY in MANY counselors
Both meaningless
People weak — many counselors bad

content: According to Proverbs 11:24, one SCATTERS yet INCREASES; another WITHHOLDS but lacks — what's the principle?
explanation: Proverbs 11:24 — 'One gives freely, yet grows all the richer; another withholds what he should give, and only suffers want.' GOD'S ECONOMIC PARADOX. Same teaching 11:25 ('the generous will be made rich'), 28:27 ('whoever gives to the poor will not lack'). NOT a guarantee of wealth — a guarantee of spiritual richness.
options: Greed gains (per the textual narrative)
Cooperation enriches
GENEROUS ABOUNDS — STINGY only LACKS
Hoarding is wise

content: According to Proverbs 11:25, the one who HELPS others — what happens?
explanation: Proverbs 11:25 — 'Whoever brings blessing will be enriched, and one who waters will himself be watered.' Like 'reap as you sow' (Gal 6:7). Applies to wealth, words, love. UNIVERSAL PRINCIPLE: what you give, you receive.
options: GENEROUS prospers; one who WATERS others is WATERED
Loses all (according to the text's framing)
Less thanked (as described in the passage)
Nothing (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Proverbs 12:25, what does ANXIETY do to the heart? What does a GOOD word do?
explanation: Proverbs 12:25 — 'Anxiety in a man's heart weighs him down, but a good word makes him glad.' Mental power of WORDS: a GOOD WORD can reverse anxiety. Same idea 15:13 (glad heart = bright face), 17:22 (joyful heart = good medicine). Encouragement is a SPIRITUAL MINISTRY.
options: Anxiety = strength; good = weakness
Anxiety = joy; good = sadness
Both = change (per the textual narrative)
ANXIETY weighs the HEART DOWN — a GOOD WORD makes it GLAD

content: According to Proverbs 13:11, how does dishonest gain compare? Slow earning?
explanation: Proverbs 13:11 — 'Wealth gained hastily will dwindle, but whoever gathers little by little will increase it.' NOT saying poor = holy, rich = evil — says HOW you gain MATTERS. Hasty gain (fraud + gambling + scams) → quickly LOST; slow earning (labor + saving) → lasting.
options: Dishonest = lasting; slow = fast (as described in the passage)
Wealth gained HASTILY (dishonestly) DWINDLES — gathering little BY LITTLE INCREASES
Both lost (per the textual narrative)
Both kept (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 13:20, who WALKS with WISE? Who is COMPANION to FOOL?
explanation: Proverbs 13:20 — 'Whoever walks with the wise becomes wise, but the companion of fools will suffer harm.' Spiritual TRANSMISSION principle: friends INFLUENCE more than we think. Same teaching 1 Cor 15:33 ('bad company corrupts good character'). NOT avoid fools entirely — but don't make them CLOSE COMPANIONS.
options: Wise = sad; fool = glad (according to the text's framing)
Both same (per the textual narrative)
Whoever WALKS with the WISE becomes WISE — companion of FOOLS suffers HARM
Wise = hated; fool = loved (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 14:29, what about one SLOW to ANGER? Hot-tempered?
explanation: Proverbs 14:29 — 'Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly.' Anger itself is not sin — but QUICK anger = lack of SELF-CONTROL. Same teaching 16:32 (slow to anger better than mighty), James 1:19 (slow to anger). 'EXALTS' = displays, makes evident.
options: SLOW to anger has GREAT UNDERSTANDING; HASTY temper EXALTS folly
Both wise (per the textual narrative)
Both foolish (as described in the passage)
Both same (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 15:33, what does FEAR of the LORD teach? Where does HUMILITY lead?
explanation: Proverbs 15:33 — 'The fear of the LORD is instruction in wisdom, and humility comes before honor.' Parallel: FEAR → WISDOM; HUMILITY → HONOR. The KEY principle: to be exalted, you must first be HUMBLED. Same teaching 18:12, 22:4. Jesus said Lk 14:11 ('whoever humbles himself will be exalted').
options: Theology — books (according to the text's framing)
Teaches WISDOM; HUMILITY comes BEFORE HONOR
Military — victory (as described in the passage)
Money — wealth (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Proverbs 16:32, who is BETTER than a MIGHTY man? Who is BETTER than a CITY conqueror?
explanation: Proverbs 16:32 — 'Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty, and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city.' Famous verse. SELF-CONTROL > physical strength. The hardest conquest = OVER YOURSELF. Same teaching 1 Cor 9:25-27, Gal 5:22-23 (self-control is fruit of Spirit).
options: Heavy laborer — Strategist (as described in the passage)
Obedient — Wall-breacher (according to the text's framing)
Brave — Crafty (per the textual narrative)
Whoever is SLOW to anger > MIGHTY man — one who RULES HIS SPIRIT > one who takes a CITY

content: According to Proverbs 17:22, what is a JOYFUL heart? An UPSET spirit?
explanation: Proverbs 17:22 — 'A joyful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.' MODERN MEDICINE confirms: prolonged stress + sadness → physical illness. Proverbs said this 3000 years ago. Reason to cultivate spiritual joy is not just feeling good — but TOTAL HEALTH.
options: Good MEDICINE — a CRUSHED spirit DRIES bones
Money — sad is health
Glory — sad is humility
Natural — sad is spiritual

content: According to Proverbs 19:17, one who is GENEROUS to the POOR — to whom does he lend?
explanation: Proverbs 19:17 — 'Whoever is generous to the poor lends to the LORD, and he will repay him for his deed.' REVOLUTIONARY concept: ACTION toward the POOR = ACTION toward GOD. Same teaching as Mt 25:40 ('as you did to one of the least of these my brothers, you did to me'). GUARANTEED return: God WILL REPAY.
options: Lends to angel
Lends to family
Lends to the LORD — God will REPAY
Lends to teacher
tags: Give to poor = lend to God

content: According to Proverbs 20:7, how does the righteous walk? What about his children?
explanation: Proverbs 20:7 — 'The righteous who walks in his integrity — blessed are his children after him!' MULTI-GENERATIONAL impact. PARENTS' righteous lives = blessing on CHILDREN. NOT a guarantee children won't have issues — guarantees GOOD SEED has been sown. Same idea Deut 7:9, Ps 112:1-2.
options: Walks fast — wise children
Beautifully — pretty children
Far — strong children (per the textual narrative)
RIGHTEOUS walks in INTEGRITY — BLESSED are his CHILDREN after him

content: According to Proverbs 21:1, the king's HEART in the LORD's hand is LIKE what?
explanation: Proverbs 21:1 — 'The king's heart is a stream of water in the hand of the LORD; he turns it wherever he will.' Same idea 16:9 (LORD directs steps), 19:21 (LORD's purpose stands). Whether GOOD or EVIL, kings are IN GOD'S HAND — he bends their will. E.g., Cyrus permits Israel's return (Isa 44:28-45:1).
options: Like CHANNELS of WATER — he TURNS it WHEREVER he wills
Like trees — growing (per the textual narrative)
Like rock — unchanging
Like clouds — flying (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 22:7, the rich rule whom? What is the BORROWER to the LENDER?
explanation: Proverbs 22:7 — 'The rich rules over the poor, and the borrower is the slave of the lender.' Economic reality. WARNING: debt is NOT NEUTRAL — it creates a POWER RELATIONSHIP. Same teaching: avoiding debt = freedom. NOT saying lending is wrong — saying know the relationship and be cautious.
options: Rule farmers — borrow wise
RICH rule POOR — BORROWER is SLAVE to LENDER
Rule army — borrow weak
Rule priest — borrow guest

content: According to Proverbs 23:4-5, do NOT toil to acquire WEALTH; wealth is LIKE what?
explanation: Proverbs 23:4-5 — 'Do not toil to acquire wealth; be discerning enough to desist. When your eyes light on it, it is gone, for suddenly it sprouts wings, flying like an eagle toward heaven.' Wealth CAN FLY AWAY — not lasting. NOT forbidding hard work — forbidding FRENZIED PURSUIT of riches (workaholism, greed).
options: Like rock — solid (per the textual narrative)
Like tree — slow growing (according to the text's framing)
Like a BIRD that FLIES TO HEAVEN — wealth SPROUTS WINGS and flies away
Like flowing water — stable (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 26:4-5, two OPPOSING instructions about FOOLS — what?
explanation: Proverbs 26:4-5 — 'Answer not a fool according to his folly, lest you be like him yourself. Answer a fool according to his folly, lest he be wise in his own eyes.' NOT contradictory — situational instructions. When engaging will lower you → DON'T ANSWER. When silence makes them proud → ANSWER. Requires DISCERNMENT.
options: Always answer; never answer (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)
Never answer; always kind (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Always argue; always endure (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
26:4 'DO NOT ANSWER fool' (lest you become like him); 26:5 'ANSWER a fool according to his FOLLY' (lest he be wise in his own eyes); need WISDOM to KNOW WHICH

content: According to Proverbs 28:1, the wicked FLEE when none pursues — what about the righteous?
explanation: Proverbs 28:1 — 'The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are bold as a lion.' SIN's psychological effect: SIN creates internal FEAR — the wicked FLEE from nothing. The righteous have NOTHING to fear → BOLD. Same teaching 1 John 4:18 ('perfect love casts out fear').
options: The righteous are BOLD as a LION
Righteous sleep well
Righteous are rich
Righteous smile

content: According to Proverbs 2:16-19, wisdom delivers from the ADULTERESS — those who go to her?
explanation: Proverbs 2:16-19 — 'So you will be delivered from the forbidden woman, from the adulteress with her smooth words... for her house sinks down to death, and her paths to the departed; none who go to her come back, nor do they regain the paths of life.' The consequences of adultery are unrecoverable. Same warnings Pr 5:1-14, 6:32-35, 7.
options: Become rich (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Learn wisdom (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Receive miracles (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
NONE who go to her come BACK — nor reach the PATHS of LIFE; her house sinks to DEATH — her tracks to the SHADES

content: According to Proverbs 3:11-12, what is the LORD's DISCIPLINE? Why?
explanation: Proverbs 3:11-12 — 'My son, do not despise the LORD's discipline or be weary of his reproof, for the LORD reproves him whom he loves, as a father the son in whom he delights.' Quoted in Heb 12:5-6 → NT. DISCIPLINE = a SIGN of FATHER'S LOVE, not of rejection. Same teaching as Rev 3:19 ('those whom I love, I reprove').
options: Punishment — God angry (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
DO NOT DESPISE the LORD's DISCIPLINE + do not LOATHE his REPROOF; for the LORD REPROVES him whom he LOVES — as a father the son in whom he delights
Random — meaningless (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Test — only for weak (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Proverbs 5:3-6, what do the LIPS of the adulteress drip first? End: bitter as what + sharp as what?
explanation: Proverbs 5:3-6 — 'For the lips of a forbidden woman drip honey, and her speech is smoother than oil, but in the end she is bitter as wormwood, sharp as a two-edged sword. Her feet go down to death; her steps follow the path to Sheol.' HONEY + OIL = sweet + slippery. WORMWOOD + SWORD = bitter + deadly. The PATTERN of adultery: pleasant START + tragic END.
options: Honey + clear water — milk + oil (according to the text's framing)
Honor + wealth — disgrace + poverty (as described in the passage)
HONEY + her mouth SMOOTHER THAN OIL; in the END BITTER as WORMWOOD, SHARP as a TWO-EDGED SWORD; her feet GO DOWN to DEATH
Sweet + warm — heat + burn (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Proverbs 6:30-35, the THIEF who steals when hungry — outcome? The ADULTERER with another's wife?
explanation: Proverbs 6:30-35 — 'People do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy his appetite when he is hungry, but if he is caught, he will pay sevenfold... He who commits adultery lacks sense; he who does it destroys himself. He will get wounds and dishonor, and his disgrace will not be wiped away. For jealousy makes a man furious, and he will not spare when he takes revenge. He will accept no compensation; he will refuse though you multiply gifts.' Adultery cannot be repaid like theft.
options: Thief only PAYS 7-FOLD; ADULTERER is FOOLISH + DESTROYS HIMSELF — no price will the JEALOUS HUSBAND ACCEPT
Both pardoned (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Both killed (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Thief killed — adulterer pardoned (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 8:22-31, when does WISDOM claim to have been with God?
explanation: Proverbs 8:22-31 — 'The LORD possessed me at the beginning of his work, the first of his acts of old. Ages ago I was set up, at the first, before the beginning of the earth... When he established the heavens, I was there... then I was beside him, like a master workman, and I was daily his delight, rejoicing before him always, rejoicing in his inhabited world and delighting in the children of man.' WISDOM personified = God's companion at creation. Col 1:15-17 applies this to Christ. Theological debate: is WISDOM = pre-incarnate Christ? Or personified attribute?
options: At earth's foundation (according to the text's framing) (per the original content) (per the textual narrative)
The LORD POSSESSED me at the BEGINNING of his work — BEFORE the creation — I was beside him as a MASTER WORKMAN, daily his delight, rejoicing before him + especially in the children of man
At Abraham's call (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
At Israel's redemption (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 8:35-36, who FINDS wisdom finds what? Who SINS against wisdom?
explanation: Proverbs 8:35-36 — 'For whoever finds me finds life and obtains favor from the LORD, but he who fails to find me injures himself; all who hate me love death.' STRONG STATEMENT: WISDOM = life itself. NO neutral zone — you EITHER seek wisdom OR choose death. Same teaching Deut 30:15-20.
options: Find money — poor (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Find strength — weak (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
Whoever FINDS wisdom finds LIFE + obtains FAVOR from the LORD; he who SINS against wisdom INJURES his soul; all who HATE wisdom = LOVE DEATH
Find name — shame (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Proverbs 14:31, who OPPRESSES the POOR does what to their Maker? Who is GENEROUS to the needy?
explanation: Proverbs 14:31 — 'Whoever oppresses a poor man insults his Maker, but he who is generous to the needy honors him.' STRONG concept: how you treat the POOR directly affects your RELATIONSHIP with GOD. Because God MADE both rich and poor (Pr 22:2).
options: OPPRESSING the POOR = INSULTING his MAKER; GENEROUS to the needy = HONORS the MAKER
Oppress = prophetic; generous = careless (in the story's sequence)
Oppress = realistic; generous = foolish (according to the text's framing)
Both normal (per the textual narrative) (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 15:8, what is the wicked's SACRIFICE to God? The upright's PRAYER?
explanation: Proverbs 15:8 — 'The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD, but the prayer of the upright is acceptable to him.' SHOCK: even SACRIFICE — the holiest of OT acts — is an ABOMINATION if you live WICKEDLY. Same teaching Isa 1:11-17, Amos 5:21-24. God cares about HEART QUALITY, not religious form.
options: Both accepted (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Both rejected (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Sacrifice good; prayer useless (as described in the passage)
The SACRIFICE of the WICKED is an ABOMINATION to the LORD; but the PRAYER of the UPRIGHT is his DELIGHT

content: According to Proverbs 16:6, by what is iniquity ATONED? By what does one TURN from evil?
explanation: Proverbs 16:6 — 'By steadfast love and faithfulness iniquity is atoned for, and by the fear of the LORD one turns away from evil.' Does NOT say HUMAN love + truth atone for sin — God's (cf. 'kindness + truth' = chesed + emet in Ex 34:6). Warning: sin is NOT automatically erased through ritual. Requires REPENTANCE through fear.
options: By sacrifice — fear (as described in the passage)
By LOVE + FAITHFULNESS iniquity is ATONED; by FEAR of the LORD, one TURNS from EVIL
By works — wisdom (per the textual narrative)
By money — wealth (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 17:5, who MOCKS the POOR insults whom? Who REJOICES at calamity?
explanation: Proverbs 17:5 — 'Whoever mocks the poor insults his Maker; he who is glad at calamity will not go unpunished.' Expansion of 14:31. LAUGHING at others' misfortune = reap-as-sow principle: that disaster WILL COME to the mocker. Combines respect for HUMAN DIGNITY (because of GOD'S IMAGE).
options: Mock poor = realistic; rejoice = right (in the story's sequence)
Both normal (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)
Whoever MOCKS the POOR INSULTS his MAKER; whoever REJOICES at calamity will NOT GO unpunished
Both wise (per the textual narrative) (as described in the passage)

content: According to Proverbs 19:21, MANY of what in a person's HEART? What ALONE STANDS in the end?
explanation: Proverbs 19:21 — 'Many are the plans in the mind of a man, but it is the purpose of the LORD that will stand.' Harmonizes with 16:9 (man plans - LORD directs), 21:1 (king's heart in LORD's hand). HUMANS plan freely — GOD rules outcomes. This verse is COMFORTING: God's PLAN WILL STAND regardless of human action.
options: Emotions — moods stand (according to the text's framing)
Ideas — minds stand (per the textual narrative)
Ambitions — plans stand (as described in the passage)
Many PLANS in a man's HEART; but only the LORD's PURPOSE will STAND

content: According to Proverbs 20:9, who can say what about HEART + sin?
explanation: Proverbs 20:9 — 'Who can say, "I have made my heart pure; I am clean from my sin"?' RHETORICAL — implies NO ONE. The point: no one is truly self-cleansed. Same teaching 1 Kgs 8:46, Isa 53:6, Rom 3:23 ('all have sinned'). Need a SAVIOR outside oneself.
options: WHO can say: 'I HAVE MADE MY HEART PURE; I AM CLEAN FROM MY SIN' — implying NO ONE can claim this
I have cleansed (according to the text's framing)
I have controlled (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates)
I am perfect (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)

content: According to Proverbs 21:13, what about one who CLOSES EARS to the POOR's CRY?
explanation: Proverbs 21:13 — 'Whoever closes his ear to the cry of the poor will himself call out and not be answered.' REAPING WHAT YOU SOW. UNIVERSAL principle: you DON'T listen to others — none listen to you. Mt 7:2 same idea. Especially for the POOR — because they have NO power.
options: Will be praised (according to the text's framing)
ALSO will CRY OUT — but NOT be HEARD
Will be blessed (as described in the passage)
Will be rich (per the textual narrative)

content: According to Proverbs 21:30-31, what wisdom CONTRADICTS the LORD? On battle day, horse prepared — but VICTORY from where?
explanation: Proverbs 21:30-31 — 'No wisdom, no understanding, no counsel can avail against the LORD. The horse is made ready for the day of battle, but the victory belongs to the LORD.' NO strategy, weapon, or talent is ENOUGH against God. Same idea Ps 33:16-17, 1 Sam 17:47 (David vs Goliath).
options: Human wisdom — horse decides (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
Ancestral wisdom — health decides (as described in the passage) (per the original content)
NO wisdom, NO understanding, NO counsel can AVAIL against the LORD; the horse is made ready for battle — BUT VICTORY belongs to the LORD
King's wisdom — fate decides (according to the text's framing) (as the text indicates)

content: According to Proverbs 22:28, do NOT MOVE what your ANCESTORS set?
explanation: Proverbs 22:28 — 'Do not move the ancient landmark that your fathers have set.' LANDMARK = boundary stone. In Israel's agricultural society, landmark = boundary of inherited property from God. MOVING = theft. Deut 19:14 same teaching. Also a deeper meaning: don't change doctrine / moral principles ESTABLISHED by ancestors.
options: Move not tree (per the textual narrative)
Move not river (according to the text's framing)
Move not house (as described in the passage)
DO NOT MOVE the ANCIENT LANDMARK (BOUNDARY) which your FATHERS have SET

content: According to Proverbs 24:11-12, what about RESCUING those LED to DEATH?
explanation: Proverbs 24:11-12 — 'Rescue those who are being taken away to death; hold back those who are stumbling to the slaughter. If you say, "Behold, we did not know this," does not he who weighs the heart perceive it? Does not he who keeps watch over your soul know it, and will he not repay man according to his work?' NO 'didn't know' excuse. Silence = serious.
options: RESCUE those being TAKEN AWAY to DEATH + HOLD BACK those STUMBLING to slaughter; if you say 'WE DID NOT KNOW' — does not he who WEIGHS the HEART perceive it?
Leave them — fate (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)
Pray only — no action (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
Hide — avoid trouble (according to the text's framing) (per the original content)

content: According to Proverbs 27:6, what does a FRIEND do? An ENEMY?
explanation: Proverbs 27:6 — 'Faithful are the wounds of a friend; profuse are the kisses of an enemy.' PARADOX: PAIN from FRIEND > SWEETNESS from ENEMY. A TRUE friend tells the TRUTH despite the pain. Same idea 27:5 ('open rebuke is better than hidden love'). Connected to Judas kissing Christ (Mt 26:48-49).
options: Both flatter (as described in the passage)
Friend's WOUNDS are FAITHFUL — but enemy's KISSES are DECEITFUL
Both rebuke (per the textual narrative)
Both silent (according to the text's framing)

content: According to Proverbs 30:7-9, Agur asks God for 2 things — what?
explanation: Proverbs 30:7-9 — 'Two things I ask of you; deny them not to me before I die: Remove far from me falsehood and lying; give me neither poverty nor riches; feed me with the food that is needful for me, lest I be full and deny you and say, "Who is the LORD?" or lest I be poor and steal and profane the name of my God.' Unusual prayer: NOT for wealth — for ENOUGH.
options: Long life + wealth (as described in the passage) (as the text indicates) (in the context of the passage)
Wisdom + strength (according to the text's framing) (per the original content) (per the textual narrative)
(1) REMOVE far from me FALSEHOOD + LIES; (2) give me NEITHER POVERTY NOR RICHES — feed me with FOOD that is needful — lest I be FULL and DENY you, or be POOR and STEAL + profane God's NAME
Glory + fame (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence) (cross-referenced with related passages)

content: According to Proverbs 31:30, what is CHARM? BEAUTY? Which woman is to be PRAISED?
explanation: Proverbs 31:30 — 'Charm is deceitful, and beauty is vain, but a woman who fears the LORD is to be praised.' Conclusion of the 'EXCELLENT WIFE' poem (31:10-31). NOT saying CHARM + BEAUTY are bad — saying they DON'T LAST. The LASTING quality is FEAR of God. Same teaching 1 Pet 3:3-4 (imperishable beauty of gentle spirit).
options: Both important (as described in the passage)
Both lasting (according to the text's framing)
Both fake (per the textual narrative) (in the story's sequence)
CHARM is DECEITFUL + BEAUTY is VAIN; but a woman who FEARS the LORD is to be PRAISED
tags: Beauty fades - fear of LORD praised
